caption: Fixes a countermarch geometry bug (#668) in the reform PR #565 landed for #552: after a drilled about-face, re-squaring the grid to the new heading used to rotate every soldier's slot by a single rigid 180 deg turn -- a point reflection that swapped each soldier to the OPPOSITE FLANK (left-right side) of the block, not just the opposite rank. The staged regiment is the same 40 spearmen at 9 files (4 full ranks + a 4-man partial rank) from #565's demo: the about-face holds every soldier's position (unchanged from the true-conversio fix), then the reform now countermarches each soldier straight to the new rank on its OWN side -- the leftmost file stays the leftmost file throughout, only trading front for rear -- instead of crossing to the mirror-image file. Verified by per-soldier state dump: soldier 0 (originally leftmost) and soldier 8 (originally rightmost) each keep their own flank across the whole about-face + reform.
